description: Native app surfaces gating (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): the `android` and `ios` target platforms are validated and gated on the general matrix. This spec is deterministic on EVERY host — it asserts the always-SKIP phase gates (android+browser -> A5, ios -> A4), which return before any SDK/emulator detection, so no host lazily installs a toolchain or boots an emulator here. (Native android app runs — which can PASS on a capable host under A3b — are exercised in the apps-android group's KVM jobs, not here.)

tests.description: An android context with a browser step is SKIPPED pointing at phase A5 (mobile web on Android). This gate fires before any SDK detection, so the outcome is deterministic on every host and never triggers a toolchain install or emulator boot.
An ios target context is SKIPPED pointing at phase A4 (iOS app surfaces). Validated and gated now so A4 is purely additive.
